verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine without its own transmembrane domain. It reaches the plasma membrane surface only when covalently tethered to transmembrane anchor proteins—GARP (glycoprotein-A repetitions predominant) on regulatory T cells and LRRC33 on myeloid cells—via disulfide bonds. This surface display is cell-type restricted and entirely dependent on the presence and expression of these TM partner anchors. The default/canonical state is secretion into extracellular matrix as part of a latent complex (LLC) with LTBP. Surface accessibility is documentedand stable (via covalent tethering) in specific immune cell types, but not constitutive across all cell types.
